data.body:
[G3 container review — parent 4fcd, child a36b] Child coverage: CLEAN, no findings.

4-bucket audit of parent 4fcd's acceptance/success criteria against THIS child (with sibling cross-check across the full roster: 22bf, 5a6c, a36b):

- Parent SC1 / AC1 ("a mechanism DETECTS material drift in the code the review was grounded against — resolved edit-set / cited files"): w.r.t. THIS child = PARTIALLY. a36b is the over-invalidation (false-positive) facet of the same root cause: its AC1 scopes freshness to "the resolved edit-set / cited files rather than the whole HEAD sha" and AC2 requires that "a relevant change (to the cited/edit-set files) STILL invalidates" — i.e. it exercises/requires the same grounded-code drift mechanism. FULL coverage is provided by sibling 22bf (AC: "binds a SIGNED {path: content-hash} map of the dependency set (file_impact ∪ review-cited files); whole-HEAD exact-match staleness check is removed"). No gap.

- Parent AC2 ("drift invalidates/flags the attestation, prompting re-review; cheap enough not to tax hot paths"): w.r.t. THIS child = PARTIALLY. a36b's AC2 (relevant change still invalidates → no regression of the under-invalidation/re-review goal) and AC4 (friction reduced; no full re-review for an unrelated commit) bear on the invalidate→re-review + cost dimension. FULLY covered by sibling 22bf ("cheap — sub-ms for a handful of files; no claim hot-path regression") and refined by 5a6c (narrowed re-review). No gap.

- Parent AC3 ("tested: code drift after a signed review → attestation no longer valid"): w.r.t. THIS child = PARTIALLY. a36b's test AC ("review at X, commit a cited file, claim is blocked") is exactly the parent AC3 scenario, plus the inverse FP test (commit an unrelated file → claim succeeds). FULLY covered by sibling 22bf's test AC (commit a dependency file → claim blocked; delete → blocked; empty-set → any commit blocks). No gap.

Coverage map (parent criterion → covering sibling):
- SC1/AC1 → 22bf (full, content-hash drift detection); a36b (partial — exercises grounded-code scoping via the FP facet); 5a6c structural (Story-2 consumer).
- AC2 → 22bf (full, invalidate + cheapness); a36b (partial — still-invalidate + friction); 5a6c (remedy refinement).
- AC3 → 22bf (full); a36b (partial — cited-file-blocked + unrelated-succeeds tests); 5a6c (narrowed-path tests).

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the union of children, and this child introduces no coverage hole. Empty findings for G3 on this pairing.
